subject: math
description: Combine two-dimensional figures to form a given composite figure. Figures used to form a composite shape are limited to triangles, rectangles and squares.

subject: math
description: Identify the attributes of a single object that can be measured such as length, volume or weight.

subject: math
description: Directly compare two objects that have an attribute which can be measured in common. Express the comparison using language to describe the difference.

subject: math
description: Express the length of an object, up to 20 units long, as a whole number of lengths by laying non-standard objects end to end with no gaps or overlaps.

subject: math
description: Given a group of up to 20 objects, count the number of objects in that group and represent the number of objects with a written numeral. State the number of objects in a rearrangement of that group without recounting.

subject: math
description: Given a number from 0 to 20, count out that many objects.

subject: math
description: Identify positions of objects within a sequence using the words “first,” “second,” “third,” “fourth” or “fifth.”

subject: math
description: Compare the number of objects from 0 to 20 in two groups using the terms less than, equal to or greater than.

subject: math
description: Recite the number names to 100 by ones and by tens. Starting at a given number, count forward within 100 and backward within 20.

subject: math
description: Represent whole numbers from 10 to 20, using a unit of ten and a group of ones, with objects, drawings and expressions or equations.

subject: math
description: Locate, order and compare numbers from 0 to 20 using the number line and terms less than, equal to or greater than.

subject: math
description: Explore addition of two whole numbers from 0 to 10, and related subtraction facts.

subject: math
description: Add two one-digit whole numbers with sums from 0 to 10 and subtract using related facts with procedural reliability.
